hero_badge: Curated AI coding skills from practitioners
hero_description: A collection of structured workflows and skills for AI agent-based software development — curated from various practitioners.
collections_description: Each collection is a set of skills from one practitioner — full guide, workflows, and skill details.
collections_next_description: Skills from other practitioners will be added here. Stay tuned.
collection_mattpocock_description: Structured workflow from idea to ship for AI agent-based software development. Main build chain, on-ramps, and Smart Zone concept.
collection_davidondrej_description: Personal agent skills across orchestration, research, thinking/docs, ops, and skill authoring. Educational catalog with per-skill compatibility status for Hermes adaptation.
david_document_title: AI Skills Guide — David Ondrej Collection
david_hero_description: 28 personal agent skills across 5 categories, sourced from davidondrej/skills. This is an educational catalog: each skill carries a compatibility status separating what exists upstream from what is safe to adapt into Hermes.
david_notice_body: This is a rolling sanitized mirror without tags or releases. Many skills assume personal paths, specific agents (Pi/Codex), or paid vendor APIs. Treat each skill's compatibility status and risk as guidance — do not bulk-install upstream as-is. Original MIT license and attribution are preserved.
david_detail_not_found_body: The skill you are looking for is not in this catalog.
david_install_description: 9 first-wave skills are adapted for Hermes Agent under skills/davidondrej/. The other 19 are catalog-only for now.
david_footer: Based on David Ondrej's personal skills (github.com/davidondrej/skills, MIT). Educational reference; adaptation is curated, not a raw mirror.
david_overview_chain_description: Upstream does not ship one official pipeline like Matt. The repo is category-based building blocks. The chain below is this guide's recommendation so first-wave skills can be used end-to-end: set context → capture vision/gaps → research if needed → verified execution → sustain sessions & schedules.
david_overview_chain_step_1: Install folder-scoped context (CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md) + setup-help for new tools.
david_overview_chain_step_2: brain-to-docs extracts vision/decisions into README + ADRs; level-up maps knowledge gaps.
david_overview_chain_step_3: research-prompt writes a self-contained deep-research brief (optional vendor deep-research).
david_overview_chain_step_4: Long work → goal-loop (5-part contract + validate). Short work → normal session + anti-sleep if needed.
david_overview_chain_step_5: handoff when context fills; agent-self-scheduling for heartbeats/cron; effective-agent-skills when authoring skills.
david_overview_problem_1_title: Project vision still in someone's head
david_overview_problem_1_description: No README/ADR/folder context; decisions vanish across sessions.
david_overview_problem_2_description: Research briefs are not self-contained; researchers keep asking for context.
david_overview_problem_3_description: Agent loops run without validate commands, constraints, or done criteria.
david_overview_problem_4_description: Full sessions without state handoffs; repeating tasks without a scheduler/heartbeat.
david_overview_categories_description: Official groupings from davidondrej/skills — a shelf of building blocks, not a pipeline.
david_flow_title: Recommended Flow: Context → Capture → Research? → Execute → Sustain
david_flow_description: Click a node for detail. A practical path across first-wave skills + sustain on-ramps, synthesized from David's skills (not an official upstream diagram).
david_flow_disclaimer: Note: upstream README only describes five category shelves. This flow is a guide recommendation so users can implement the skills correctly.
david_flow_onramp_handoff: Context full / focus change → state handoff
david_flow_node_context: Folder-scoped CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md + one-step-at-a-time setup-help. Multi-session context agents need.
david_flow_node_capture: brain-to-docs: Q&A → README vision + ADRs. level-up: 7 adaptive questions → knowledge log + learning plan.
david_flow_node_research_gate: Branch: does the decision need structured external evidence?
david_flow_node_research: research-prompt: one self-contained paragraph (goal, sub-Qs, constraints, finding format). DeepAPI/deep-research optional.
david_flow_node_work_gate: Branch: >30 min, clear stop condition, agent-ready repo?
david_flow_node_goal: goal-loop: Objective · Constraints · Validate · Stop · Document. anti-sleep keeps the Mac awake during the loop.
david_flow_node_session: Normal interactive work; setup-help if a new tool/config appears mid-way.
david_flow_node_handoff: Compress the session into state (not orders). Capture why + traps. Paste into a fresh session.
david_flow_node_schedule: Camp A: external cron/loop. Camp B: Hermes cron + zero-token/heartbeat. Stay silent when idle.
david_flow_node_author: effective-agent-skills: progressive disclosure, anatomy, anti-patterns, testing, security when writing SKILL.md.
david_workflows_description: Concrete scenarios composing David's skills. Step order is a guide recommendation — take the subset that fits.
david_workflow_1_description: A fresh agent needs folder context + written vision before coding.
david_workflow_1_why: Without CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md and ADRs, agents re-ask and invent assumptions.
david_workflow_2_description: Owner architects via agents rather than hand-writing code — map gaps first.
david_workflow_2_why: level-up produces an honest plan; teach (Matt) fills gaps, not the reverse.
david_workflow_3_description: Turn a fuzzy research need into a one-paragraph brief + report.
david_workflow_3_why: A zero-context researcher/AI can run without multi-round Q&A.
david_workflow_4_description: Migrations, coverage lifts, refactors with a test contract.
david_workflow_4_why: goal-loop needs a 5-part contract + validate command; anti-sleep prevents Mac sleep mid-loop.
david_workflow_5_description: Periodic checks, digests, watchdogs without empty noise.
david_workflow_5_why: Scheduler + silent-when-idle; Hermes zero-token for pure watchdogs.
david_workflow_6_description: Session almost full or focus changed.
david_workflow_6_why: State handoffs (done/partial/not-started + why + traps) beat imperative checklists.
david_workflow_7_description: Codify a repeatable workflow into a portable SKILL.md.
david_workflow_7_why: effective-agent-skills + folder context prevent bloated, non-triggering, or path-hardcoded skills.
david_concepts_description: Principles that make David's skills fit a personal agent stack — more than a file dump.
david_concept_1_body: Upstream groups skills into five categories. Users assemble their own workflows. This guide only recommends the most common chain.
david_concept_2_body: Skills load name+description first (~100 tokens), body on match, references/scripts on demand. Install many skills without permanent context bloat.
david_concept_3_body: Every /goal needs Objective, Constraints, Validate command, Stop condition, and Document. Without them, autonomous loops become expensive drift.
david_concept_4_title: Camp A vs Camp B scheduling
david_concept_4_body: One-shot agents (Claude/Codex/Pi) need an external clock. Hermes has built-in cron, zero-token mode, chaining, and a ban on self-spawning cron from scheduled jobs.
david_concept_5_body: Write what is true / partial / missing; capture why and dead ends. The fresh agent chooses actions after verifying files — not from an imperative checklist.
david_concept_6_body: Compatibility + risk separate "exists on GitHub" from "safe for Hermes". First-wave first; vendor/personal-path skills stay catalog-only.
footer_attribution: AI coding skills reference guide. Each collection's content refers to the original author's work — respect their license and attribution.
footer_mattpocock: Based on Matt Pocock's AI Skills system. Reference guide.
matt_hero_description: 22 composable skills for real engineering (not vibe coding), sourced from mattpocock/skills v1.1. Main build chain, on-ramps, Smart Zone, and idea-to-ship workflows.
overview_build_chain_description: This is the main pipeline from idea to merge-ready code. Each step produces an artifact that becomes the input for the next step. The entire system is designed to keep the agent in the "Smart Zone" — never exceeding ~120k token context where output quality degrades.
overview_smart_zone_description: "AI is not a super-powered developer. It's a new starter with no memory." — Matt Pocock. A large context window (1M tokens) doesn't mean everything is useful. After ~120k tokens, the agent's attention diffuses and output degrades. The entire skill system is designed to keep the agent in the Smart Zone.
overview_compact_description: Summarize current session → return to Smart Zone. For single-threaded long work.
overview_handoff_description: Split to new session → keep current session pure. For parallel concerns.
overview_problem_1_description: Agent misinterprets or doesn't focus on the given requirement.
overview_problem_2_description: Output too long, no clear domain model, high cognitive load.
overview_problem_3_description: Hard-to-trace bugs, no tests, trial-and-error approach.
overview_problem_4_title: Codebase becomes a ball of mud
overview_problem_4_description: Unclear architecture, poor module boundaries, accumulating technical debt.
overview_invocation_description: Skills are divided into two types based on who invokes them:
overview_invocation_model: — invoked automatically by the agent when needed (e.g., /tdd)
flow_description: Click a node to see details. This is the main pipeline for all feature work.
flow_canvas_hint: Click a node to see details
flow_triage_description: Bug enters → state machine → main flow
flow_improve_description: Scan code rot → fix → main flow
flow_node_grill: Deep interview + domain modeling. Update CONTEXT.md and ADRs.
flow_node_multi: Is this a multi-session project or a quick feature?
flow_node_to_spec: Synthesize to spec: Problem Statement, Solution, User Stories, Decisions, Out of Scope.
flow_node_to_tickets: Break spec into tracer-bullet tickets with blocking edges. To tickets.md or a tracker.
flow_node_implement: Write code, driven by /tdd for test-first development.
flow_node_tdd: Red → Green. Test at seams (public interfaces). Refactor moved to code-review (v1.1).
flow_node_code_review: Two axes: Standards (Fowler smells) + Spec (matches spec/ticket?).
flow_node_commit: Code passes both review axes. Merge and ship.
flow_node_triage: ON-RAMP: Bug enters through state machine to main flow.
flow_node_improve: ON-RAMP: Scan shallow→deep opportunities, then implement.
workflows_description: Common scenarios and recommended skill sequences. Each workflow is designed to keep the agent in the Smart Zone (~120k tokens).
workflow_1_description: Main pipeline from idea to merge-ready code.
workflow_1_why: Each step produces an artifact that becomes the input for the next. No step can be skipped without sacrificing output quality.
workflow_2_description: Systematic debugging with 6 phases — feedback loop FIRST, hypothesis SECOND.
workflow_2_why: Jumping to hypothesis without a feedback loop is failure mode #1. Phase 1 (build feedback loop) is THE SKILL — the rest is mechanical.
workflow_3_description: Identify shallow modules and deepen them gradually.
workflow_3_why: If the codebase is garbage, AI produces garbage. Fix the output first, THEN improve the system that produces it. Run weekly or after a development surge.
workflow_4_description: Turn a messy backlog into agent-ready work via state machine.
workflow_4_why: Without triage, issues pile up without clarity. The state machine ensures each issue has exactly one category + one state — no ambiguity.
workflow_5_description: Shortcut for small features where scope is 100% clear.
workflow_5_why: Not every feature needs a full PRD. If scope is small and can be described in 1-2 sentences, go straight to grill → implement.
workflow_6_description: Handoff → prototype in new session → handoff back learnings.
workflow_6_why: When grilling finds questions that need code to answer (UI that must be seen, state machine that must be tried), don't pollute the current session. Use a fresh session's full context window to explore, compress learnings, pass back.
workflow_7_description: Bridge context from one AI tool to another via markdown.
workflow_7_why: Handoff document = markdown. Portable to any agent: Claude Code → Codex → Copilot → any agent. Enables adversarial review (different agents review each other) and tool diversity.
concepts_description: Mental models underlying this skill system.
concepts_deep_shallow_p1: "AI is not a super-powered developer. It's a new starter with no memory." Every time you spawn an agent, it's like Guy from Memento entering a codebase: "Okay, I'm here, what am I doing?" Your codebase is the BIGGEST influence on AI output — bigger than prompts or AGENTS.md.
concepts_deep_shallow_p2: Deep module = lots of implementation behind a simple interface. Shallow module = interface almost as complex as its implementation. With deep modules, AI can read interface types and understand what a module does — without digging into implementation.
concepts_grey_box_description: Deep modules create natural seams. You own the interface, AI owns the implementation. Tests keep it honest. You can look inside if you want — to apply taste, influence outcome, or improve performance — but as long as tests pass, you don't NEED to care.
concepts_progressive_description: Give every module its own folder with a clear public interface. AI can see all services in the file system, read their types, and understand what they do — without digging into implementation. Interface on top, details inside. Reduced cognitive burnout: instead of hundreds of interrelated modules, you only keep 7-8 chunks in your head.
concepts_smart_zone_p1: Claude Code's context window = 1 million tokens. But there's a sweet spot: at the start of the window, attention relationships aren't strained, the agent can focus better. After ~120k tokens, responses gradually get dumber — Matt personally feels it enters the "dumb zone."
concepts_compact_description: Summarize large conversation → return to smart zone. Like sediment — layers of previous conversations. Good for single-threaded debugging.
concepts_handoff_description: Take a slice of context → give to a new session → keep current session pure. Doesn't clobber progress. Cross-agent portable (Claude → Codex → Copilot).
concepts_vertical_description: Every issue must cut ALL layers end-to-end (UI → API → DB → Tests). Never break work horizontally — that creates handoff problems and partially finished work that can't be verified.
concepts_horizontal_note: → Handoff problems, partially finished work, nothing verifiable until step 4
concepts_vertical_note: → Each slice includes schema+API+UI+tests, independently verifiable
concepts_tracer_label: ← one tracer bullet cuts all layers →
concepts_afk_description: Each vertical slice is marked with a work mode. This determines whether you can leave the agent working alone, or need to be present for checkpoints.
concepts_afk_text: Agent can implement without further human input. Spec is clear enough, no unresolved design decisions. Prefer this mode.
concepts_hitl_text: Agent needs a human checkpoint — design review, architectural decision, or unresolved judgment call. Can't be left to run alone.
concepts_afk_note: /to-tickets gives each ticket blocking edges. Any ticket whose blockers are done sits on the frontier — several agents can work them in parallel.
concepts_seams_description: Seam = public interface where behavior can change without editing in place. Interface IS the test surface — callers and tests cross the same seam. If you need to test private implementation, the module boundary is wrong.
concepts_leading_description: Start every instruction with the most important word — a compact concept from pretraining that anchors behavior. One token replaces a paragraph. Examples: "tight" loop (not "fast, deterministic, low-overhead" loop), "red-green" (not "write test first then implement"), "tracer bullet" (not "thin vertical slice that proves a path works end-to-end").
concepts_context_description: Keep CONTEXT.md as a glossary only — vocabulary definitions, not narrative or spec. ADRs only for decisions meeting 3 criteria: hard to reverse + surprising without context + real trade-off. Prototypes are deleted once the question is answered. Every file in context must earn its place.
installation_description: These skills are available for several AI coding agents. Choose your agent and follow the installation steps.
installation_notes_hermes: Choose per-skill install (tap) or activate all at once (external_dirs).
installation_attribution_suffix: (MIT License). Original content kept intact — only frontmatter format adjusted per agent.
document_title: AI Skills Guide — Matt Pocock Workflow
david_install_notes_hermes: 9 first-wave skills under skills/davidondrej/. Install per skill via tap, or enable all with external_dirs.
david_install_attribution_suffix: (MIT License). Curated adaptation, not a raw mirror — check each skill’s compatibility status.
installation_notes_skills_sh: One command via skills.sh (npx skills). The CLI detects your agent (Claude Code, Codex, OpenCode, Cursor, etc.) and installs skills to the right location. Pick skills in the interactive prompt.
david_install_notes_skills_sh: Install upstream directly via skills.sh. Many skills are personal/vendor-specific — check prerequisites and catalog compatibility before bulk install. The Hermes first-wave bundle above stays the curated path.
